content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods (ICM, RND) can be mapped to the three progressive learning stages of Three-Stage Progressive Reinforcement Learning to create a self-scaffolding curious agent that automatically adjusts its intrinsic reward strategy as it accumulates environment knowledge. In this framework, an agent uses RND's uncertainty-based exploration in the first exploration stage, ICM's feature prediction error to refine causal understanding in the second consolidation stage, and a tuned baseline curious agent's sparse intrinsic reward to prioritize high-value novel regions in the final specialization stage.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
